stories.story_title: As a developer I can run `docker-compose up` and all services start
As a developer I can run `pytest services/ -x -q` and all tests pass
As a developer I can hit http://localhost:8080/health and get status=ok
As a developer I can run the data-model on port 8011 (start.ps1)
As a developer I can use the .env.example as a complete checklist
As a developer, every PR triggers ruff lint. Zero lint errors = green.
As a developer, every PR triggers mypy type check. No unresolved types.
As a developer, every PR triggers pytest. No test failures = merge allowed.
As a developer, main branch push triggers axe-core a11y check on frontend.
As an operator I can run infra/phase1-marco/main.bicep and get 7 Cosmos containers

As an operator collector-schedule.yml triggers nightly at 02:00 UTC
As an operator PUBLIC_APP_URL and PUBLIC_API_URL are read from env vars;

As an operator ACA_ALLOWED_ORIGINS is seeded from the Phase 1 free hostname
As a client using Mode A (delegated) I see a PASS/FAIL/WARN status

As a client with PASS_WITH_WARNINGS I can still proceed with collection
As an operator I can pass --preflight-only to the collector to validate
As the system I collect all Azure resources via Resource Graph in < 60s
As the system I save inventory to Cosmos with partition_key=subscriptionId
As the system I capture: resource type, name, SKU, region, resource group, tags

As the system I capture: date, MeterCategory, MeterName, resourceGroup,
As the system I handle rate limiting (429) with exponential backoff + retry
As the system I collect all Advisor recommendations across all categories
As the system I collect Policy compliance state (compliant / non-compliant counts)
As the system I collect network signals: NSG rule counts, private DNS zones,
As the system I update scan status in Cosmos: queued -> running -> succeeded/failed
As the system I write stats to the scan record (inventoryCount, costRows, advisorRecs)
As the API I expose GET /v1/scans/:scanId so the frontend can poll status
As the system, after mark_collection_complete sets status=collected, the analysis
As the system I load all 12 rules from ALL_RULES and run each in sequence
As the system I handle a rule failure in isolation (one rule crash does not
As the system I persist each Finding to Cosmos with full schema:
As the system I update AnalysisRun status: queued -> running -> succeeded/failed
As the system I write findingsSummary to the analysis run record
As a Tier 1 client calling GET /v1/findings/:scanId I receive findings
As a Tier 1 client I do not receive narrative or deliverable_template_id
As a Tier 2 client I receive the full finding including narrative and
As a Tier 3 client I receive the full finding including deliverable_template_id
As the red-team agent I can assert that Tier 1 tokens never leak
R-01 Dev Box auto-stop: returns finding when annual Dev Box cost > $1,000
R-02 Log retention: returns finding when annual LA cost > $500 in non-prod
R-03 Defender mismatch: returns finding when annual Defender cost > $2,000
R-04 Compute scheduling: returns finding when annual schedulable compute > $5,000
R-05 Anomaly detection: returns finding for each category with z-score > 3.0
R-06 Stale environments: returns finding when >= 3 App Service sites exist
R-07 Search SKU oversize: returns finding when annual Search cost > $2,000
R-08 ACR consolidation: returns finding when >= 3 registries exist
R-09 DNS sprawl: returns finding when annual DNS cost > $1,000
Unit test for R-01 devbox_autostop: fixture with Dev Box cost > $1,000 -> finding
Unit test for R-02 log_retention: fixture with LA cost > $500 -> finding
Unit test for R-03 defender_mismatch: fixture with Defender cost > $2,000 -> finding
Unit test for R-04 compute_scheduling: fixture with schedulable > $5,000 -> finding
Unit test for R-05 anomaly_detection: fixture with z-score > 3.0 -> finding
Unit test for R-06 stale_environments: fixture with >= 3 App Services -> finding
Unit test for R-07 search_sku_oversize: fixture with Search cost > $2,000 -> finding
Unit test for R-08 acr_consolidation: fixture with >= 3 registries -> finding
Unit test for R-09 dns_sprawl: fixture with DNS cost > $1,000 -> finding
Unit test for R-10 savings_plan_coverage: fixture with compute > $20,000 -> finding
Unit test for R-11 apim_token_budget: fixture with APIM + OpenAI -> finding
Unit test for R-12 chargeback_gap: fixture with total cost > $5,000 -> finding
Negative tests for each rule: below-threshold fixture -> no finding returned
FindingsAssembler unit test: mock rule list -> correct Cosmos upsert payload
As a client I can sign in via Microsoft Identity (any Microsoft tenant);
As the API I validate the JWT on every authenticated endpoint using
As the API I extract subscriptionId from the session (stored after connect);
As a client I can connect my Azure subscription (POST /v1/auth/connect)
As a client I can disconnect (POST /v1/auth/disconnect) and all access
As a developer auth.py is reworked: MSAL authority=common, no EsDAICoE
As the frontend LoginPage, sign-in CTA calls MSAL.js with
POST /v1/auth/connect         -- Connect Azure subscription
POST /v1/auth/preflight       -- Run pre-flight probes
POST /v1/auth/disconnect      -- Disconnect subscription
POST /v1/collect/start        -- Trigger collector job (was /v1/scans)
GET  /v1/collect/status       -- Poll collection progress (was /v1/scans/:scanId)
GET  /v1/reports/tier1        -- Tier 1 findings report (was /v1/findings/:scanId)
POST /v1/billing/checkout     -- Stripe checkout (tier2 or tier3 in body)
GET  /v1/billing/portal       -- Stripe billing portal redirect URL
POST /v1/webhooks/stripe      -- Stripe event handler
As the gateway I validate JWT signature on all /v1/* routes
As the gateway I cache /v1/entitlements response for 60s per subscriptionId
As the gateway I return 403 with TIER_REQUIRED error when a Tier 1
As the gateway I enforce subscription key throttling (100 req/min default)
As the gateway I forward X-Subscription-Id header to the API on all calls
GET  /v1/admin/kpis
GET  /v1/admin/customers?query=
POST /v1/admin/entitlements/grant
POST /v1/admin/subscriptions/:subscriptionId/lock
POST /v1/admin/stripe/reconcile
GET  /v1/admin/runs?type=scan|analysis|delivery

frontend/src/app/api/adminApi.ts -- admin API calls:

POST /v1/checkout/tier2 returns a Stripe checkout session URL
POST /v1/checkout/tier3 returns a Stripe checkout session URL
mode=subscription creates a Stripe subscription (Tier 2 monthly)
mode=one_time creates a one-time session (Tier 2 one-time, Tier 3)
Checkout metadata contains subscriptionId and analysisId
As a client with a coupon code I can enter it at Stripe checkout
settings.py has STRIPE_COUPON_ENABLED: bool = True field
As an admin I can create Stripe promotion codes for trial clients
checkout.session.completed -> write entitlement to Cosmos, trigger delivery
invoice.paid -> renew Tier 2 subscription entitlement for next period
customer.subscription.updated -> update Tier in Cosmos clients container
customer.subscription.deleted -> downgrade to Tier 1 in Cosmos unless Tier 3 permanent
All webhook events written to payments container for audit trail
GET /v1/entitlements?subscriptionId=X returns { tier, validUntil, features[] }

12 Jinja2 template folders exist in services/delivery/app/templates/
Each folder has main.bicep and README.md (Bicep Phase 1 only;

ZIP is assembled with findings.json manifest at root

ZIP is uploaded to Azure Blob Storage with 24h SAS URL

Stripe webhook events are logged with event type + subscriptionId (hashed)
Alert: API service 5xx rate > 5% in 5 minutes -> PagerDuty / email

Alert: Anomaly detection rule fires -> owner notified (Story R-05 output)
